title: Quick check: Step caps and circuit breakers

questions.prompt: What's the minimum layered safety set every production agent needs?

choices.text: Step cap (max iterations) + per-tool timeouts (prevent hung tools) + per-run wall-clock cap (catches what step cap misses) + circuit breakers on external deps + cost caps at user/feature/API-key levels. Each catches a different failure mode; together they make 'agent ran overnight burning $X' impossible.

questions.explanation: Single safety controls fail in unexpected ways. Layered defenses (step cap, timeouts, wall-clock, circuit breakers, cost caps) catch different failure modes and create a real safety net. The API-key cap is the airbag — application-level controls should catch issues earlier, but the cap is the last resort.

questions.prompt: An external API your agent depends on goes down. Without circuit breakers, what happens?

choices.text: Every agent run hits the dead API, times out (e.g. 30s), retries, times out again — burning latency and tokens cascading across users. Circuit breakers stop calling the dead dependency for a cooldown period, return a clear 'feature degraded' result, and probe periodically to detect recovery.
Nothing — agents are resilient by default.

questions.explanation: Without circuit breakers, dependency outages cascade through every agent run. The breaker fails fast when the upstream is sick, freeing capacity and giving users a quick degraded response. When the dep recovers, the breaker probes and re-opens traffic automatically.
